claims.text: apm install resolves dependencies in apm.yml, downloads with transitive resolution and content-addressed cache, runs a built-in security scan, and deploys primitives to detected targets.
apm install writes apm.lock.yaml so the next install on any machine reproduces the same files.
With no arguments installs everything from apm.yml; with PACKAGE_REF arguments adds those packages to apm.yml and installs only what was added.
http:// deps are refused unless --allow-insecure or --allow-insecure-host HOSTNAME is passed.
--frozen flag enables lockfile-only install that refuses to resolve anything new and fails if apm.yml and apm.lock.yaml have drifted.
--force overwrites locally-authored files on collision and bypasses the security scan's critical-finding block.
--target accepts: copilot, claude, cursor, opencode, codex, gemini, windsurf, agent-skills with 'all' expanding to every harness except agent-skills.
-g/--global flag installs to user scope (~/.apm/) instead of current project; MCP servers deploy only to global-capable runtimes.
--parallel-downloads N defaults to 4 and can be disabled with 0.
--skill NAME flag installs only named skills from a SKILL_BUNDLE package and selection is persisted to apm.yml and apm.lock.yaml.
--mcp NAME adds an MCP server entry to apm.yml with --transport inferred from --url or post-`--` argv when omitted.
Exit 0 = success; exit 1 = install failure including security scan blocks, auth errors, resolution errors; exit 2 = usage error.
apm install --frozen fails if direct dependency is absent from apm.lock.yaml or lockfile is missing, exiting with code 1.
Security scan source files for hidden Unicode and tag-character/bidi-override patterns before deployment; critical findings block and exit 1.
ref: field accepts semver ranges for git-source deps; APM runs git ls-remote, picks highest matching tag, and pins tag plus commit SHA in apm.lock.yaml.
claims.section: Behavior - Semver ranges on git deps
